articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Widespread Concern:** 82% of landlords are worried about the Renters’ Rights Act, with nearly half (44%) expressing significant anxiety about its impact.  
- **Portfolio Shrinkage Ahead:** 75% of landlords feel regulations hinder portfolio growth, leading 49% to consider selling or reducing their holdings in the next two months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords need to prioritize improving EPC ratings to attract tenants, especially with a looming requirement for a C rating by 2030.  
- Rising energy costs due to the Iran War have heightened tenant demand for energy-efficient properties as a way to save money.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Less than 25% of UK landlords believe that proposed planning reforms will benefit their businesses.  
- Landbay's findings reveal a significant lack of confidence in the effectiveness of these reforms among property investors.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- Over 58% of hidden leaks in rental properties come from outside, emphasizing the need for landlords to maintain external pipework.
- Neglecting outdoor plumbing could lead to costly repairs, highlighting the importance of comprehensive property maintenance for landlords.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters’ Rights Act, effective May 1, 2026, will introduce significant reforms to the Private Rented Sector, aiming to enhance the balance between landlord and tenant rights.  
- Landlords should prepare for new regulations and oversight coming with the Act, which will fundamentally change their responsibilities and obligations.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 84% of landlords are not prepared for the upcoming Renters’ Rights Act, set to take effect on May 1.
- With 58% facing a high risk of non-compliance, many rental properties may not meet the new legal requirements.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are prioritizing portfolio restructuring over expansion as rental growth slows, indicating a shift in focus to align with shifting demand.  
- The latest ONS data highlights the need for landlords to optimize their financing and property holdings in response to the changing rental landscape.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey indicates that landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.  
- The growing dominance of landlords is raising concerns over accessibility for new homeowners.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scotland's landlords urge the next government to implement a formal strategy to acknowledge and support the significance of the private rented sector (PRS).  
- A cohesive plan is essential to stem the outflow of landlords and sustain the housing market in Scotland.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting in May, HMRC mandates tax advisor registration for conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams, with penalties reaching £10,000 for non-compliance.
- Landlords must ensure their tax advisors are registered to avoid significant financial repercussions.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council claims that implementing licensing won't drive up rental prices for landlords.  
- Landlords face a £694 fee per property, with the council not anticipating cost recovery from this expense.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook ruled out the implementation of rent controls, emphasizing a focus on market solutions.  
- This decision aims to reassure landlords and promote stability within the UK rental market.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council aims to impose stricter regulations to curb the unchecked rise of Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs).  
- New detailed guidelines are being formulated to establish clear standards for HMO landlords.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long campaign is launching to educate tenants on leveraging their rights under the Renters Rights Act.  
- The initiative aims to empower renters to better advocate for themselves in the housing market.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The newly implemented Renters Rights Act is leading to a significant increase in Section 21 evictions, as anticipated by property expert Paul Shamplina.  
- This surge highlights the challenges renters face amid evolving legislation in the UK housing market.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- **Rising Rents**: New data from Hamptons shows rental prices are increasing as landlords prepare for the upcoming Renters Rights Act.  
- **Market Shift**: The trend suggests a potential shift in the rental landscape, with landlords possibly adjusting prices ahead of regulatory changes.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act is set to significantly impact the hobbyist landlord market in the UK, potentially leading to a decline in smaller-scale property owners.  
- With just 10 days until the law is enacted, experts warn that the changes could reshape the rental landscape and drive amateur landlords out of the market.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly venturing into property investment, undeterred by regulatory hurdles and additional taxes.  
- This trend reflects a growing interest among younger generations in building wealth through real estate, even amidst challenging market conditions.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Sellers are facing challenges in the current market, often needing to lower asking prices or withdraw listings due to increased failed sales.
- House Buyer Bureau's Chris Hodgkinson highlights the tough conditions for sellers as the real estate landscape shifts.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is increasing, boosting Inheritance Tax receipts.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC is intensifying its review of property valuations for Inheritance Tax, leading to a rise in cases sent to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).  
- This heightened scrutiny is contributing to increased Inheritance Tax revenue for the UK government.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos

- The unusual property marketing incident raises eyebrows as it showcases the tenant unexpectedly dozing in bed during the home's sale presentation.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers

Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.

articles.ai_summary:

- This practice is criticized as an unfair "hidden tax," adding financial strain to the property purchasing process.

articles.ai_summary:

- David Alexander of Lomond emphasizes the strategic growth of the company in the northern UK market.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 90% of newly built homes are never listed on the open sales market, highlighting a significant disconnect in property availability.  
- Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto, emphasizes the hidden nature of these homes in the current real estate landscape.
